introduction:
A Death Certificate in Delhi is issued by the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD), New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC), or Delhi Cantonment Board depending on the area of occurrence. It is governed by the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969.

This certificate is mandatory for insurance claims, property mutation, bank account settlement, pension/gratuity processing, and succession proceedings. Delhi offers online registration through the e-District Delhi portal and the CRS portal.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Register within 21 days of death (free)
  • 21 days to 30 days: late fee + Registrar permission
  • After 1 year: SDM / Area Magistrate order required
  • First certified copy is FREE
  • Track at edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  • Helpline: 1076 (MCD) / 155370 (e-District)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Note: Carry originals; submit self-attested copies.
Proof of Death
  • MCCD from hospital / attending doctor
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • FIR / Post-mortem report (unnatural death)
ID of Deceased
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
For Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit on stamp paper
  • SDM / Magistrate order (after 1 year)
  • Non-availability certificate
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-District Delhi / CRS

Delhi provides online registration through e-District Delhi and CRS.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Portal
    Go to edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create account using Aadhaar / mobile. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select "Death Certificate"
    Navigate to Certificate Services → Death Certificate.
  4. Fill Form
    Enter deceased's details, date/place/cause of death, informant info.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID, informant docs. Max 200 KB
  6. Submit
    Note Application ID from SMS.
  7. Verification
    Area Registrar verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: Download certificate from e-District portal or UMANG app once approved.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at MCD / NDMC Office

Visit the MCD Zonal Office, NDMC Sub-Division, or SDM office.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Office
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 9 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect Form
    Get Death Registration Form (Form No. 2) — free.
  3. Fill & Attach
    Fill accurately, attach photocopies of all documents.
  4. Submit & Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement with registration number.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit e-District Delhi.

Download Certificate

Download from e-District portal or UMANG app.

Legal Use

Use for insurance, property, bank, pension, and succession matters.

Grievance

Call 1076 (MCD) or 155370 (e-District).
